skill.name: azure-hosted-copilot-sdk
skill.description: Build and deploy GitHub Copilot SDK apps to Azure. Scaffold new projects, integrate Copilot SDK into existing apps, configure Azure BYOM (Bring Your Own Model), and deploy with Azure Developer CLI.
skill.summary: Build and deploy GitHub Copilot SDK apps to Azure with scaffolding, SDK integration, and Azure model hosting.
skill.author: microsoft

security_audit.summary: This Microsoft skill contains only Markdown documentation files with instructional bash commands for Azure deployment workflows. All 194 static findings are false positives: backtick patterns are documentation examples (not code execution), URLs are official Microsoft/GitHub references, and environment variable access is legitimate for Azure credential management. No executable code or malicious patterns detected.

content.user_title: Deploy Copilot SDK Apps to Azure
content.value_statement: Developers struggle to build and host AI-powered apps with GitHub Copilot SDK. This skill scaffolds projects, integrates Copilot SDK into existing codebases, configures Azure models, and deploys to Azure Container Apps.
actual_capabilities: Scaffold new Copilot SDK projects using azure-samples/copilot-sdk-service template
Integrate Copilot SDK into existing Node.js, Python, Go, or .NET applications
Configure Azure BYOM (Bring Your Own Model) with DefaultAzureCredential authentication
Deploy Copilot-powered apps to Azure Container Apps with Azure Developer CLI
Set up GitHub token flow with Key Vault for secure credential management
Discover available models via listModels() and Azure CLI deployment queries
limitations: Requires Docker Desktop installed and running for local development
Requires GitHub CLI with copilot scope for authentication
Azure BYOM only supports o-series and gpt-5 family models (not gpt-4o)
Bearer tokens expire after 1 hour and require manual refresh in production

use_cases.title: Greenfield Copilot App Development
use_cases.description: Start a new project with Copilot SDK integration from scratch
use_cases.target_user: Developer building a new AI-powered chat application

use_cases.title: Add Copilot to Existing Application
use_cases.description: Integrate Copilot SDK endpoints into an existing web application without rewriting infrastructure
use_cases.target_user: Team lead modernizing an existing codebase with AI features

use_cases.title: Enterprise Azure Model Deployment
use_cases.description: Deploy Copilot apps using Azure AI Foundry or Azure OpenAI with managed identity authentication
use_cases.target_user: Enterprise architect requiring compliant self-hosted AI infrastructure

prompt_templates.title: Scaffold a new Copilot SDK project
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a new Copilot SDK project on Azure. I want to build a chat application from scratch with React UI and Express API.

prompt_templates.title: Integrate Copilot SDK into existing app
prompt_templates.prompt: Add Copilot SDK to my existing Express.js application. I have a /api/routes folder and want to add an AI chat endpoint at /api/chat.

prompt_templates.title: Configure Azure BYOM with specific model
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up Azure BYOM for my Copilot SDK app. I have an Azure OpenAI resource with an o4-mini deployment at https://myresource.openai.azure.com. Use managed identity authentication.

prompt_templates.title: Deploy existing Copilot SDK app to Azure
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a working Copilot SDK app locally. Help me deploy it to Azure Container Apps with proper infrastructure using azd.

output_examples.input: Scaffold a new Copilot SDK project
output_examples.output: Created new project structure with azure-samples/copilot-sdk-service template. API service ready at /api with Express endpoints. Web UI running on React/Vite. Infra configured for Azure Container Apps deployment.

output_examples.input: Add /api/chat endpoint to existing Express app
output_examples.output: Added @github/copilot-sdk dependency. Created chat route with session management and streaming response. Configured GitHub token authentication for local development.

best_practices: Read AGENTS.md in scaffolded templates to understand file purposes before modifying
Use context7 MCP tools to get current Copilot SDK documentation and code examples
Increase nginx proxy_read_timeout to 300s for multi-turn AI sessions to prevent 504 errors
anti_patterns: Do not run azd init inside existing projects - it overwrites files. Scaffold to temp directory first.
Do not use wireApi 'responses' with store:false - it breaks multi-turn tool calls. Use 'completions' instead.
Do not nest apiVersion at top level for Azure provider - it must be under azure: { apiVersion: '...' }

faq.question: What models work with Azure BYOM in Copilot SDK?
faq.answer: Only o-series (o3, o3-mini, o4-mini) and gpt-5 family models support BYOM. Models like gpt-4o and gpt-4.1 do not work because they do not support the SDK's encrypted content format.

faq.question: How do I fix 'gh auth token' failures?
faq.answer: Run 'gh auth login' to authenticate, then 'gh auth refresh --scopes copilot' to add the required copilot scope. GitHub CLI must be installed.

faq.question: Why does my Copilot SDK session hang or timeout?
faq.answer: Check nginx.conf.template and increase proxy_read_timeout to 300s. The default 60s timeout causes 504 errors for long-running AI sessions.

faq.question: How do I discover available Azure model deployments?
faq.answer: Use 'az cognitiveservices account deployment list --name <resource> --resource-group <rg>'. The SDK listModels() only returns GitHub models, not Azure deployments.

faq.question: What is the difference between GitHub default and Azure BYOM?
faq.answer: GitHub default uses GitHub's hosted models with automatic authentication. Azure BYOM lets you use your own Azure OpenAI or AI Foundry deployments with DefaultAzureCredential for enterprise control.

faq.question: How do I handle token expiration for Azure BYOM?
faq.answer: Bearer tokens expire after 1 hour. For production, get a fresh token per request using DefaultAzureCredential.getToken(). Do not cache tokens for long-running sessions.
